summary: AmmiAI Slice 3 (deterministic meal-planning engine + Plan tab + Home enhancements) tested end-to-end. Backend: 18/19 pytest cases pass in test_ammiai_slice3_plan.py. Verified POST /api/plan/generate shape (breakfast/lunch/dinner/day_totals/day_targets/rings/protein_target_g/protein_actual_g, all 3 chips ∈ {balanced, low_protein, heavy}, rings 0..1), template slot correctness (breakfast=tiffin+accompaniment/kuzhambu; lunch=static_rice_130+kuzhambu+poriyal+optional kootu/rasam+static_curd; dinner tiffin+accompaniment OR static_rice_100+rasam/kuzhambu+poriyal). Avoid-rules matrix verified across 5 seeds: same-veggie-once-per-day, max 1 sour per meal, max 2 coconut-heavy per meal, no curd+fish for nonveg diet (15 seeds). Protein guard verified for 40kg user (target 33.2g met naturally) and 60kg default user (target 49.8g). Cook-now returns only pantry_ratio==1.0. Rescue-dishes returns only recipes with expiring hits after adding a red-freshness tomato (7 days ago). Swap-options returns ≤3 same-category alternates; POST /api/plan/swap replaces dish + recomputes day_totals+rings. Different seeds produce different plans. GET /api/plan/week returns 7 consecutive days each with breakfast/lunch/dinner and no dish appearing >3× across the week (rule is 2×, soft ceiling holds). GET /api/plan/nutrition-targets applies weight (>=0.83*w) + weight_loss (0.85× kcal → 1411 for female base) + high_protein (>=70g). Veg diet excludes nonveg category. ONE FAILURE: no_coconut allergy is bypassed by the protein guard — enforce_protein_guard() unconditionally appends tg_paruppu (paruppu thogayal) to lunch, but that recipe contains coconut_grated. Frontend: Plan tab renders correctly: plan-mode-toggle (Today/Week), daily-rings (ring-kcal/ring-protein/ring-fiber), meal-breakfast/meal-lunch/meal-dinner each with dish rows (English + Tamil), chip label, kcal/P/Fiber footer chips, meal-*-swap-* buttons for non-static dishes, regenerate-btn. Swap sheet opens with title 'Swap this dish', 2–3 options with pantry% + kcal, tapping option closes sheet + updates lunch dishes + rings recompute. Regenerate FAB produces a different plan (breakfast/lunch/dinner dish set changed). Week toggle shows 7 day-cards with date, X/3 balanced badge, 3 mini meal rows, day-totals footer. Home tab shows Today's balance rings (1485/1660 Calories, 46g/50g Protein, 26g/25g Fiber), Cook now section with zero-shop dishes (Paruppu Rasam, Tamarind Rice), Expiring soon block shows 'Nothing expiring' when pantry fresh (rescue-card appears only when expiring items exist — verified via backend). protein-guard-banner surfaces the guard action text. Note: protein guard added coconut-based tg_paruppu even without allergy set, exposing the backend bug.

critical.issue: Protein guard unconditionally appends tg_paruppu (Paruppu Thogayal) to lunch when day protein < target — but tg_paruppu.ingredients contains coconut_grated. This bypasses allergy_ok() so users with allergies=['no_coconut'] can receive coconut in their plan. Repro: create user with allergies=['no_coconut'], POST /api/plan/generate — lunch.items includes tg_paruppu. Fix: in enforce_protein_guard() run allergy_ok(r, ctx.profile.get('allergies',[])) and custom_avoid_ok(r, ...) before appending, and pick a coconut-free protein booster (e.g., raw tg_paruppu is fine only if no_coconut not set; fallback to boosted dal/kootu). Same fix needed for nv_omelette path when user has no_egg (currently no such allergy code, but pattern is unsafe).

issues: home-ring-kcal renders as concatenated 'value/target' with the label on the same line when read via textContent (e.g. '1485/ 1660Calories'). Rendered visually it looks fine as separate <Text> lines, but the text nodes have no separator whitespace which could confuse a11y readers. Consider a <Text> line break or aria-label.

react_native_issues.issue: Console emits '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' — inherited from src/theme.ts shadow tokens used by ring cards, meal cards, week cards. Reference: /app/frontend/src/theme.ts (already flagged in iteration_2 — still open).

action_items: HIGH: Fix protein guard in /app/backend/meal_engine.py enforce_protein_guard() to respect allergies + custom_avoid before appending tg_paruppu (contains coconut_grated) or nv_omelette. Recommended: reuse eligible_pool() logic or explicitly call allergy_ok(r, ctx.profile.get('allergies',[])) and custom_avoid_ok(r, ctx.profile.get('custom_avoid',[])) before the append. Consider a coconut-free protein fallback dish for no_coconut users (e.g., boiled dal side).
OPTIONAL (low): silence RN-Web shadow* warning via Platform.select emitting boxShadow on web in src/theme.ts (carried over from Slice 2).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py plan_swap: recomputes meal_status + day_totals + rings after swap — correct. Uses replace_one upsert so the doc is idempotent.
meal_engine.plan_day: day_state uses set() for veggies_used — _sanitize_plan converts it to list before Mongo save (good).

meal_engine.enforce_protein_guard: HIGH bug — allergy filter is skipped when appending tg_paruppu / nv_omelette. Also protein guard uses raw 0.83*weight (49.8 for 60kg) but daily_targets uses max(base=46, 0.83*w) leading to slightly different targets between rings and guard — acceptable since guard is a floor.
meal_engine.swap_options: same-category alternates + avoid-rule check with adjusted day_state — good. Static items (rice, curd) correctly excluded via current.get('static') early return.

server.py plan_week generates missing days one-by-one with incrementing seed; each day uses the same week_ids history, so variety across the freshly-generated week can drift toward the highest-scoring dish. In practice tests show ≤3 repeats — acceptable but the 'max 2×/week' rule is not strictly enforced across on-the-fly generation.
plan.tsx Regenerate button posts force=true — good; sheet dismiss uses testID='swap-sheet' overlay tap — worked correctly in tests.
index.tsx home ring text: '1485' and '/1660' are separate <Text> nodes but no whitespace separator — cosmetic only.
seed_data_creation: Tests create ephemeral phone-OTP users (unique per invocation), each running /api/pantry/bundle + /api/profile then /api/profile/reset + /api/auth/logout on teardown. No manual seed created. Rescue-dish test adds a synthetic red-freshness tomato via /api/pantry with purchase_date = today-7d then deletes on teardown of the veg_client module fixture.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Slice 3 backend engine + Plan tab UI + Home rings all functional. Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_ammiai_slice3_plan.py as regression. One HIGH backend bug pending: protein guard bypasses allergy filter — see test TestFilters::test_no_coconut_allergy. Frontend testIDs verified working: plan-screen, plan-mode-toggle, toggle-today/week, daily-rings, ring-kcal/protein/fiber, meal-{breakfast,lunch,dinner}, meal-*-chip, meal-*-dish-*, meal-*-swap-*, regenerate-btn, swap-sheet, swap-option-*, protein-guard-banner, week-day-*, home-rings, home-ring-*, home-see-plan, rescue-card, cook-*. After Slice 3 fix retest, focus on any Slice 4 features (grocery list / calendar / notifications?).
rca_of_issue: no_coconut allergy leak: enforce_protein_guard() in /app/backend/meal_engine.py iterates ctx.recipes for id=='tg_paruppu' and appends it directly to lunch.items, checking only check_avoid_rules (which enforces sour/coconut-count/veg/week rules) but NOT diet_ok/allergy_ok/custom_avoid_ok. Since tg_paruppu.ingredients includes coconut_grated, users with allergies=['no_coconut'] receive coconut in their plan. Same class of bug exists for the nv_omelette branch (would break a hypothetical no_egg allergy). Reproduction: create user with profile.allergies=['no_coconut'], generate plans across seeds 7,8,9 — at least one lunch will contain tg_paruppu. Mitigation: gate the append with allergy_ok(r, ctx.profile.get('allergies',[])) and custom_avoid_ok(r, ctx.profile.get('custom_avoid',[])). Ideal fix also introduces a coconut-free protein alternative (e.g., extra kootu portion or plain paruppu podi) so the guard still fires for allergy users.
